response:
Nurse Emily organizes a creative writing workshop for the older adults in her care facility, ensuring that every resident has the opportunity to participate. She divides the 24 residents into groups of 6, resulting in 4 groups in total. To support the creative process, Emily prepares 3 writing prompts for each group, which amounts to 12 prompts for the session. 

In addition to the initial prompts, Emily has prepared an extra set of 10 prompts to be shared equally among all the groups. This ensures that if a group finishes their initial prompts, they can continue with the extra ones. The library at the facility serves as a resource for residents, and Emily also keeps a well-organized library of writing ideas to draw from. 

By combining the initial and extra prompts, Emily needs to have a total of 12 + 10 = 22 writing prompts prepared for the session. 

The END.
